business.type_label: organic farm online shop, restaurant & agriculture training

engine.ladder_reasoning: Homepage and product pages are grids of organic products with photos and prices; browsing the catalogue and shop/restaurant offering IS the visitor's decision. It is really a storefront, so browsing-as-decision maps to place over thing.

goal.one_action: browse and buy organic products (add to cart / checkout)

structure.archetype_note: WooCommerce catalog+checkout with founder/philosophy About branch and a contact form; classic producer-storefront, not a single conversion page.

mapped.observed_as: About the Organic Farm / メッセージ / Founder message

unmapped.observed: Recommended / Popular / Featured Products grids with live prices + cart
unmapped.why_no_fit: Canonical sections have no product-catalogue-with-checkout concept; these are commerce listings, not marketing sections

proposed_new.name: storefront-checkout
proposed_new.evidence: WooCommerce cart(0), sign in/sign up account, prices shown (120–1,400 ฿), on-site checkout for one-time organic goods

confidence.why: fetch_quality full; commerce, forms, nav, and languages all captured clearly

gap_findings: site's job is a storefront (browse catalog + cart + checkout + account login), not one conversion action — falls outside the 5-engine single-action model
shop-and-restaurant + agriculture-training are secondary offers with their own destinations, unrepresentable as one page's single CTA
